title: Chaetopappa aurea
intro: Chaetopappa aurea, also known as yellow Chaetopappa or golden Chaetopappa, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Chaetopappa aurea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the most common herbal preparations being a tea made from the dried flowers and leaves, as well as a tincture made from the root and stem.
taxonomy: Chaetopappa aurea is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Heliantheae. Chaetopappa aurea is native to the southwestern United States.
